description: Claude Code safety enforcement plugin generalizing agent-manifesto L1 hooks

assumptions.content: PreToolUse hooks are the only reliable mechanism for blocking unsafe tool execution. Deny rules bypassable (PoC 3), CLAUDE.md normative only.
Plugin hooks merge with host project hooks via parallel execution. Conflict resolution: deny > defer > ask > allow.

validity.sourceRef: Claude Code hooks docs, CC-H6, PD-14

assumptions.content: CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT paths in hooks.json are resolved at runtime, making plugin portable.
jq is available on target systems. Without jq, all hooks fail silently (PD-71 fail-open), resulting in zero enforcement.
Fixed regex patterns for destructive commands achieve less than 5 percent false positive rate across diverse projects.
Filename-based secret detection (env, pem, key, credentials patterns) is sufficient without content inspection.
Test tampering detection via disable-keywords works cross-framework (Jest, Mocha, RSpec, pytest).
Plugin can protect own scripts via file guard blocking Edit/Write to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT.
POSIX shell utilities (grep -qE, sed, basename) suffice for cross-platform portability (macOS + Linux).
Blocked events must be logged from PreToolUse hook itself (PostToolUse never fires for blocked tools).
Zero-config installation is achievable with fixed universal safety patterns.
Hook stdin JSON schema (tool_input.command, tool_input.file_path) is stable across Claude Code versions.
